images.alt: The image is a call-to-action poster for urgent mutual aid. The background is a mixture of red and black with a green olive branch design on the left side. The main text, written in large, bold red letters, reads: "URGENT MUTUAL AID." Below it, in smaller white text, there is a message that reads: "Funds needed for rent and other basic essentials in Egypt for a young Palestinian survivor from Gaza!" In the top right corner, there is an illustration resembling a postage stamp with the text "CAIRO Egypt" and an image of two green pyramids against a red background with a sun. The bottom of the poster features a URL, "bit.ly/rentfund," and a QR code on the left side for easy access to the donation link.

images.alt: Woosan hugging om stage with a caption that says "just wanna be yours by sugahnspice7 (starshineandhappythoughts)". Wooyoung has lilac hair and San has oreo hair.
AO3 tags for just wanna be yours by sugahnspice7 (starshineandhappythoughts)

just wanna be yours by sugahnspice7 (starshineandhappythoughts) summary: Wooyoung closed the hotel door behind him, and the click of the lock vibrated through San as they turned to face each other. The grin on Wooyoung's face, the heated look in his eyes, was the same one he'd racked over San after the concert. The look that stripped him bare and left San instantly hot and hard between one breath and the next.

It was the same look that told San that, yes, they were actually doing this.

Wooyoung's sly grin stayed as he reached across and pulled San in for a kiss.

They'd kissed a couple times before, always drunk and full of laughter, and it had never gone any further. Those kisses had haunted him and left him craving more.

record.bridgyOriginalText: Advertising works. As @ryno points out, marketing budgets for movies are often more than the production costs. So what you get is a lot of concentrated attention and wealth. That's bad for culture and all of society really. But when their power is in manipulating us, how do you fix that except through critical thinking education and information sharing tools that leave no space for attention/opinion manipulation? This is what drives my support for media decentralization and being anti algo.

I am so porous, I know
so I try, try so hard to detach 
from the pains and the sorrows of the world.

𝘈𝘕𝘋 then I remember, 
the reeds at the river's edge stay standing
both through draught, and flood
and we bend
so that we don't break. 

— ER

and 𝘐 𝘤𝘢𝘯, too. 

— ER, just now
ㅤ

value.text:
I am so porous, I know
so I try, try so hard to detach 
from the pains and the sorrows of the world.

But then I remember, 
the reeds at the river's edge stay standing
both through draught, and flood
and we bend
so that we don't break. 

— ER

Reeds in the mist at Lough Derg, Ireland — John Quinn

images.alt: A cicada exuviae (shell shed by the cicada upon leaving the soil underground) left hanging on the bark of the tree in our K yard.
The exuviae now on a clipboard for students to observe up close.

Poem on chart paper with text including arrows and a drawn magnifying glass.
Text reads: 
“Looking Closely 
By Alexis McDonell
Look up,
Look down, 
Look ALL around!
What do you think?
What do you see?
What are your wonders?
Share them with me!”

record.text:
TB made an exciting discovery outside this afternoon: “I looked up in the tree and saw it.” 
We said “That’s using our Looking Closely skills, like our poem!”
When we told other students it was an exuviae left behind by a cicada who shed their hard skin, T added, “A snake can shed its skin, too.” 🔎

Full colour illustration of an adventuring party standing in V formation, weapons drawn. Behind them, the Dungeon Master looms, holding a grimoire. His billowing cloak surrounds them, and from beneath it the shadows of ghouls with glowing eyes loom closer. The party consists of Ravi, a human paladin holding a halberd; Muirnuir, a dwarf cleric holding a symbol of Dumathoin; Sombre, a tiefling sorcerer manipulating shadows that flow around them; Uppa, a tortle cleric holding a mace; and Alma, an elf swashbuckler holding a rapier and shortsword.

Half-body sketch, fully coloured, of two Baldur's Gate 3 adventurers standing back to back: Varian, a tanned elf with short hair and a tattoo of black tendrils flowing from their left eye to their neck, resting a longsword on their shoulder, and Akseli, a taller tiefling with grey skin and short horns, spiky black hair and piercings, wearing an embroidered green suit and holding a flute.

Bust artwork of Angharad, a tiefling woman with pale lilac skin, curly dark hair, ram's horns and blue eyes with horizontal irises. She's wearing adventurer attire with a sea green cloak fastened with a silver brooch and is petting the white pseudodragon familiar perched on her shoulder.

Close crop of a coloured sketch depicting a sweet moment between Mori and Mana. Mori has slightly messy blonde hair, lilac eyes and glasses, and he wears dark comfortable clothing. Mana has shoulder-length white hair, red irises and a smattering of freckles across her nose. She wears a comfy colour-blocked sweater. She's leaning against Mori and reaching out to caress his cheek as they gaze into each other's eyes. The background shows a green-tinted snippet of a sliding door with paper windows.

images.alt:
Screenshot reads:

'“Did you save room for dessert?” Quinn asked, and I nearly fell out of my chair.
“What?”
They blinked, and from the completely guileless expression on their face I honestly think they had no idea what they were saying. Shit, could they seriously not know? “Dessert. Did you want some? They do a really good red velvet cake here.”
“I’m not allowed. Red 40,” I explained, when they gave me the most baffled look. “It’s the dye most places use to make it look red? It’s supposed to be really bad for you, so Mom says I’m not allowed to…” 
Stars, how silly did I sound. My mom was on the run from [Agency] and the last time I’d seen her was when she’d invaded my dreams, and I was worried about her food rules. What was she going to do, ground me? Come find me when I fell asleep to berate me for daring to eat a slice of cake?"

Cake is highlighted twice.

Thanks for asking.   We're hanging in as best we can. Sending this from a parking lot on Tunnel Road under a cell phone tower, one of the few places where you can get a signal.

We took on some water, but our house didn't get severely damaged. Many houses in the neighborhood are severely damaged.  The destruction is unimaginable. 

Whatever you've seen on the national news, the reality is worse.  Power and cellphone service is out throughout the area.  They're hoping to have power restored by Friday.  That may be unduly optimistic. 

The water system is totaled.  Boil order in place.  Estimates are weeks or months before it's fully restored.  They're trucking in water and setting up distribution centers

Internet has been out but is slowly coming back.

Most roads into the area are closed, including I-40 in both directions and I-26 west to Tennessee.  This obviously severely limits getting relief crews, food, water, etc. into the area.  They've begun airlifts of food and water.  Asheville airport is closed to commercial traffic to expedite relief support.

Small towns in the area like Marshall,  Hot Springs,  Swannanoa,  Chimney Rock, Lake Lure and many others are destroyed.

Stores are running out of food.  Gas stations are running out of gas.  Fist fights are breaking out in gas stations.   Curfew in place from 7:30pm to 7:30am until further notice.  National Guard and helicopters are everywhere 

Considering what impacts other people have had, we consider ourselves very, very lucky.

Probably more info than you wanted.  We're trying to get the word out.
